summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that the questionnaire templates controller redirects to user-supplied URLs without proper validation, confirming the open redirect vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that the vulnerable controller uses URI.parse(params[:url]).path without validating the destination, allowing open redirects to external malicious sites.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully demonstrated that the vulnerable controller uses redirect_to URI.parse(params[:url]).path without validation. While the .path method extracts only the path portion, this still presents security risks through edge cases and can facilitate phishing attacks.
verification_method: The verification script runs a Ruby application that simulates the vulnerable QuestionnaireTemplatesController code. The script tests various malicious URL patterns and validates that the redirect executes without proper validation.
evidence_produced_by_run: The vulnerable code in apply() action uses redirect_to URI.parse(params[:url]).path without validation
The vulnerable code in skip() action uses redirect_to URI.parse(params[:url]).path without validation
No domain allowlisting or URL validation was observed
The code accepts arbitrary user-supplied URLs via the params[:url] parameter
Edge cases like data:// and file:// URIs could potentially be abused
limitations_or_blockers: The PoC demonstrates the vulnerability concept without a full Decidim installation
A full PoC would require setting up a complete Decidim Rails application with admin authentication
In a real attack scenario, the attacker would need template admin privileges to exploit this
The .path method mitigates some but not all redirect abuse scenarios

reproduction.source_acquisition: The PoC simulates the vulnerable code pattern. In production, run.sh would clone https://github.com/decidim/decidim and mount the repository.
